entries.answer:
**SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program)** is a federal program that provides monthly food assistance on an EBT card.

In New York, SNAP uses Broad-Based Categorical Eligibility (BBCE), which means:
- **Gross income limit:** 200% of the Federal Poverty Level
- **No asset test** in NY
- **Net income** must be at or below 100% FPL after deductions

For a household of 3, the gross income limit is approximately $53,300/year.

**How to apply:** Online at [ACCESS HRA](https://a069-access.nyc.gov/accesshra/), by phone at 718-557-1399, or in person at your local HRA office.

*Citation: 7 U.S.C. § 2014(c); NY Social Services Law § 95*

**To apply for SNAP in NYC:**

1. **Online:** [ACCESS HRA](https://a069-access.nyc.gov/accesshra/) — available 24/7
2. **Phone:** Call 718-557-1399
3. **In person:** Visit your local [HRA office](https://www.nyc.gov/site/hra/locations/locations.page)

You'll need:
- Proof of identity (ID, birth certificate, etc.)
- Proof of NYC residency (utility bill, lease, etc.)
- Proof of income (pay stubs, tax return, etc.)
- Social Security numbers for household members

Processing typically takes 30 days. Emergency (expedited) SNAP is available within 7 days if you qualify.

**Medicaid** provides free or low-cost health coverage in New York.

**Income limits (as % of Federal Poverty Level):**
- Adults (19-64): **138% FPL**
- Children (0-18): **154% FPL** (Medicaid) or up to **400% FPL** (Child Health Plus)
- Pregnant women: **223% FPL**

**NY Essential Plan:** If you earn between 138-200% FPL, you may qualify for the Essential Plan with **$0-20/month** premiums.

**How to apply:** [NY State of Health](https://nystateofhealth.ny.gov/) or call 1-855-355-5777.

*Citation: 42 USC 1396a(a)(10); NY Social Services Law Article 5, Title 11*

**EITC (Earned Income Tax Credit)** is a tax credit for working individuals and families.

NYC residents can receive a **triple credit:**
1. **Federal EITC** — up to $7,830 (with 3+ children)
2. **NY State EITC** — 30% of your federal EITC
3. **NYC Noncustodial Parent EITC** — up to 5% of federal (narrower eligibility)

**Income limits (2025 tax year, approximate):**
- Single, no children: ~$18,600
- Single, 1 child: ~$49,000
- Single, 2 children: ~$55,700
- Single, 3+ children: ~$59,900
- Married filing jointly limits are higher

You must have **earned income** and a valid **SSN**.

**How to claim:** File your federal and NY State tax return. Use [NYC Free Tax Prep](https://www.nyc.gov/site/dca/consumers/file-your-taxes.page) for free filing.

*Citation: IRC Section 32; NY Tax Law Section 606(d)*

**WIC (Women, Infants, and Children)** provides nutritious foods, nutrition education, breastfeeding support, and health referrals.

**Who qualifies:**
- Pregnant women
- Postpartum women (up to 6 months, or 12 months if breastfeeding)
- Infants and children under age 5

**Income limit:** 185% of the Federal Poverty Level. **Automatically eligible** if you receive SNAP, Medicaid, or TANF.

**How to apply:** Call 311 or visit a [NYC WIC center](https://www.nyc.gov/site/doh/health/health-topics/wic-find-a-clinic.page).

*Citation: 42 USC 1786; 7 CFR 246*

**Section 8 / NYCHA** provides rental assistance for low-income families in NYC.

**Income limits** are based on Area Median Income (AMI):
- Extremely low income (30% AMI): Priority for 75% of vouchers
- Very low income (50% AMI): General eligibility

**Important:** The NYCHA Section 8 and public housing **waitlists have been CLOSED** for several years. Monitor [nyc.gov/nycha](https://www.nyc.gov/site/nycha/eligibility/eligibility.page) for any new openings.

**Alternative:** Check [NYC Housing Connect](https://housingconnect.nyc.gov/) for affordable housing lotteries.

*Citation: 42 USC 1437f; 24 CFR 982*

**Fair Fares NYC** provides a **half-price MetroCard** for subway and eligible bus rides.

**Who qualifies:**
- NYC residents ages **18-64**
- Household income at or below **100% FPL** (e.g., ~$15,650/year for 1 person)

Seniors 65+ and people with disabilities qualify for separate MTA reduced fare programs.

**How to apply:** [ACCESS HRA](https://a069-access.nyc.gov/accesshra/) or visit your local HRA office.

*Citation: NYC Local Law (administered by DSS/HRA)*

**NYC Care** provides low-cost healthcare to NYC residents who are **uninsured** and don't qualify for or can't afford health insurance.

**Key features:**
- **No immigration status requirement** — open to undocumented residents
- No strict income limit
- Provides a primary care provider, specialty care, prescriptions, and more
- Available at all **NYC Health + Hospitals** facilities

**How to enroll:** Call **646-NYC-CARE (646-692-2273)** or visit any NYC Health + Hospitals location.

*Administered by NYC Health + Hospitals*

**IDNYC** is NYC's free municipal identification card.

**Who qualifies:** All NYC residents age **10+** (ages 10-13 need a parent/guardian).
- **No income test**
- **No immigration status requirement**
- Provides access to city services, building entry, and free museum memberships

**How to get one:** Visit an [IDNYC enrollment center](https://www.nyc.gov/site/idnyc/card/enrollment-centers.page) with proof of identity and NYC residency.

*Citation: NYC Administrative Code § 3-115*

**SCRIE (Senior Citizen Rent Increase Exemption)** freezes rent for eligible seniors.

**Who qualifies:**
- Age **62 or older**
- Combined household income **$50,000 or less**
- Must live in a **rent-regulated** apartment
- Must pay more than 1/3 of income toward rent

**How to apply:** [NYC Department of Finance](https://www.nyc.gov/site/finance/benefits/tenants-scrie.page) — online, by mail, or in person.

*Citation: NYC Administrative Code § 26-509; NY Real Property Tax Law § 467-b*

**DRIE (Disability Rent Increase Exemption)** freezes rent for people with disabilities.

**Who qualifies:**
- Must receive **disability benefits** (SSI, SSDI, VA disability, etc.)
- Combined household income **$50,000 or less**
- Must live in a **rent-regulated** apartment
- Must pay more than 1/3 of income toward rent

**How to apply:** [NYC Department of Finance](https://www.nyc.gov/site/finance/benefits/tenants-drie.page) — online, by mail, or in person.

*Citation: NYC Administrative Code § 26-510; NY Real Property Tax Law § 467-b*

**HEAP (Home Energy Assistance Program)** helps low-income households pay heating and cooling costs.

**Income limit:** Approximately 150% FPL (based on 60% of State Median Income).

**Automatically eligible** if you receive SNAP, SSI, or Temporary Assistance.

**Program periods:**
- Regular HEAP: Opens ~November 1
- Emergency HEAP: Opens ~January
- Cooling Assistance: Available in summer

**How to apply:** [ACCESS HRA](https://a069-access.nyc.gov/accesshra/) or call 212-331-3126.

*Citation: 42 USC 8621-8630; NY Social Services Law § 97*

**NYC Free Tax Prep** provides free tax preparation for NYC residents earning **$85,000 or less**.

Services include:
- Federal and NY State tax return filing
- Help claiming EITC, Child Tax Credit, and other credits
- Available in multiple languages

**How to use:** Visit a [Free Tax Prep site](https://www.nyc.gov/site/dca/consumers/file-your-taxes.page) or call 311.

*Administered by NYC Department of Consumer and Worker Protection (DCWP)*

**GetFoodNYC** connects you to free food resources across all five boroughs.

- **No income test** required
- **No ID** required at most sites
- Includes food pantries, community kitchens, and distribution sites

**How to find food:** Visit [GetFoodNYC](https://www.nyc.gov/site/foodpolicy/get-food/get-food.page) or call **311**.

You may also qualify for **SNAP** (monthly food assistance on an EBT card) — use `mygovassist check` to see if you're eligible.

**If you recently lost your job in NYC, here's what may be available to you:**

1. **Unemployment Insurance** — File at [labor.ny.gov](https://dol.ny.gov/unemployment-insurance)
2. **SNAP** — Food assistance up to $292-1,756/month depending on household size. Apply at [ACCESS HRA](https://a069-access.nyc.gov/accesshra/)
3. **Medicaid** — Free health coverage if income is below 138% FPL. Apply at [NY State of Health](https://nystateofhealth.ny.gov/)
4. **HEAP** — Help with heating/utility bills. Apply at ACCESS HRA
5. **Fair Fares** — Half-price MetroCard if income is at/below 100% FPL
6. **GetFoodNYC** — Immediate free food, no income test. Call 311

Run `mygovassist screener` for a personalized check of all programs you may qualify for.

**If you're pregnant or just had a baby in NYC:**

1. **Medicaid** — Pregnant women qualify up to **223% FPL**. Apply at [NY State of Health](https://nystateofhealth.ny.gov/)
2. **WIC** — Nutrition support for pregnant/postpartum women and children under 5. Income limit: 185% FPL. Visit a [NYC WIC center](https://www.nyc.gov/site/doh/health/health-topics/wic-find-a-clinic.page)
3. **SNAP** — Food assistance. Apply at [ACCESS HRA](https://a069-access.nyc.gov/accesshra/)
4. **Child Tax Credit** — $2,000/child when you file taxes
5. **EITC** — Earned income credit increases with children

Run `mygovassist screener` for a personalized check.

**Benefits for seniors in NYC:**

1. **SCRIE** — Rent freeze if you're 62+, income ≤$50K, in a rent-regulated apartment. Apply at [NYC Finance](https://www.nyc.gov/site/finance/benefits/tenants-scrie.page)
2. **Medicare** — Federal health coverage at 65+. [medicare.gov](https://www.medicare.gov/)
3. **SNAP** — Seniors with low income qualify. Apply at [ACCESS HRA](https://a069-access.nyc.gov/accesshra/)
4. **HEAP** — Help with heating costs. Apply at ACCESS HRA
5. **MTA Reduced Fare** — Half-price MetroCard for 65+. Apply at [mta.info](https://new.mta.info/fares/reduced-fare)
6. **IDNYC** — Free museum memberships and city services

Run `mygovassist screener` for a personalized check.

The **Federal Poverty Level (FPL)** is an income threshold set annually by HHS. Most benefit programs use it to determine eligibility.

**2025 FPL Guidelines (48 contiguous states + DC):**

| Household Size | 100% FPL | 138% FPL | 200% FPL |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | $15,650 | $21,597 | $31,300 |
| 2 | $21,150 | $29,187 | $42,300 |
| 3 | $26,650 | $36,777 | $53,300 |
| 4 | $32,150 | $44,367 | $64,300 |

For each additional person, add $5,500.

*Source: [HHS ASPE](https://aspe.hhs.gov/topics/poverty-economic-mobility/poverty-guidelines)*

I can help you find NYC benefits you may qualify for!

Run `mygovassist screener` for a guided, step-by-step eligibility check across **14 programs** including:
- **Food:** SNAP, WIC, GetFoodNYC
- **Healthcare:** Medicaid, NYC Care
- **Tax Credits:** EITC (triple credit in NYC!), Child Tax Credit
- **Housing:** Section 8/NYCHA, SCRIE, DRIE
- **Other:** Fair Fares, HEAP, IDNYC, Free Tax Prep

Or use `mygovassist check` with your details for an instant result.

**If you're struggling with rent in NYC:**

1. **SCRIE** (seniors 62+) or **DRIE** (disabled) — Rent freeze in rent-regulated apartments. Income limit: $50K
2. **Section 8 / NYCHA** — Rental assistance (waitlists currently closed, but check for openings)
3. **NYC Housing Connect** — Affordable housing lotteries: [housingconnect.nyc.gov](https://housingconnect.nyc.gov/)
4. **One Shot Deal** — Emergency rent assistance through HRA: [ACCESS HRA](https://a069-access.nyc.gov/accesshra/)
5. **311** — Call for additional rental assistance resources

Also apply for **SNAP**, **Fair Fares**, and **HEAP** to reduce other expenses.

**Benefits available regardless of immigration status in NYC:**

1. **NYC Care** — Low-cost healthcare at NYC Health + Hospitals. Call 646-NYC-CARE
2. **IDNYC** — Free municipal ID card. No immigration status check
3. **GetFoodNYC** — Free food at pantries and community kitchens. No ID needed
4. **WIC** — If pregnant or have children under 5 (income limit: 185% FPL)
5. **Emergency Medicaid** — For emergency medical conditions
6. **NYC Free Tax Prep** — If you file taxes with an ITIN

Some programs (SNAP, regular Medicaid, EITC) require US citizenship or qualified immigrant status.

Run `mygovassist screener` and set citizenship status to see which programs apply to you.
